title: Speaking - Part 2
description: Compare the photographs and answer the questions.

tasks.instructions: Compare two of the pictures, and say what the advantages and disadvantages of living in a remote community might be and what challenges people there face.

images.label: A
images.description: A small indigenous community in a densely forested region, houses built on stilts above a river, with wooden boats as the main form of transport

images.label: B
images.description: A tiny Arctic settlement surrounded by snow and ice, a handful of colourful wooden houses visible against a vast white landscape

images.label: C
images.description: A remote highland village accessible only by narrow mountain path, residents carrying supplies on their backs as there is no road access

followUp.question: Do you think remote communities should be encouraged to modernise and connect with mainstream society, or should their way of life be protected?

tasks.instructions: Compare two of the pictures, and say what motivates people to work in international development and what impact their work might have.

images.label: D
images.description: An aid worker installing a solar-powered water pump in a rural African village, surrounded by local residents watching with curiosity and interest

images.label: E
images.description: A relief worker distributing emergency food rations to a crowd of displaced people, working methodically and calmly under difficult conditions

images.label: F
images.description: A development worker teaching basic healthcare skills to local community health volunteers in a makeshift training room with hand-drawn diagrams on the walls

followUp.question: What do you think are the greatest challenges for international aid workers, and how can aid organisations work more effectively?
